title: campanula latifolia var. macrantha
intro: Campanula latifolia var. macrantha, also known as the Canterbury bell, is a flowering plant that belongs to the bellflower family (Campanulaceae) and is characterized by its large, bell-shaped flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to the presence of campanulene, a sesquiterpene compound. The health benefits of Campanula latifolia var. macrantha include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and act as a natural remedy for respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Campanula latifolia var. macrantha can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are typically used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
